articles.author: Lucas Ropek
articles.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move - TechCrunch
articles.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."
articles.content:
OpenAI’s first foray into hardware devices is reported to be a mobile smart speaker with integrated AI capabilities that can sync with ChatGPT and provide other home AI services. 
Bloomberg reported… [+2527 chars]

articles.author: Dan Goodin
articles.title: Microsoft Secure Boot has been broken for most of its existence - Ars Technica
articles.description: Old and forgotten "shims" Microsoft failed to revoke have made Secure Boot bypasses simple.
